BlogPost.title: BlogPost
BlogPost.description: Represents a single blog post or article, including its content, metadata, and references to authors and categories.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlogPost entity.

title.description: The title of the blog post.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the blog post, derived from the title.

content.description: The full body content of the blog post, possibly in HTML or Markdown format.

featuredImageUrl.description: The URL of the main image displayed with the blog post.

description.description: A short summary or excerpt of the blog post content.

publicationDate.description: The date and time when the blog post was published.

readTimeMinutes.description: The estimated reading time of the blog post in minutes.

isHeroCarouselItem.description: Indicates if this blog post should be featured in the main hero carousel on the homepage.

isFeaturedPost.description: Indicates if this blog post should be displayed in the 'Posts em Destaques' section.

authorId.description: Reference to the Author who wrote this blog post. (Relationship: Author 1:N BlogPost)

categoryIds.description: References to Categories this blog post belongs to. (Relationship: Category N:N BlogPost)

Author.title: Author
Author.description: Represents an author of blog posts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Author entity.

name.description: The full name of the author.

bio.description: A short biography or description of the author.

profileImageUrl.description: The URL of the author's profile picture.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category for grouping blog posts or products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the category, derived from the name.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

heroImageUrl.description: The URL of an image to be displayed as a hero or background for this category, especially for featured categories.

isFeatured.description: Indicates if this category should be displayed in the 'Categorias em Destaque' section.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a fashion product item from the catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

imageUrl.description: The URL of the product's image.

productExternalUrl.description: The external URL where the product can be viewed or purchased.

isCatalogItem.description: Indicates if this product should be displayed in the 'Catálogo da Fashion' carousel.

StaticPage.title: StaticPage
StaticPage.description: Represents a static content page like 'About Us', 'Contact', 'Privacy Policy', etc.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StaticPage entity (e.g., 'about-us', 'contact').

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the static page.

title.description: The title of the static page.

content.description: The full content of the static page, which can include rich text or HTML.

primaryImageUrl.description: The URL of a primary image associated with the static page, used for illustrative purposes in sections like 'Conheça a História'.

sectionName.description: Optional name of the section this page belongs to in navigation (e.g., 'Conheça mais, a Fashion', 'Suporte').

FAQ.title: FAQ
FAQ.description: Represents a Frequently Asked Question and its answer.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FAQ entity.

answer.description: The detailed answer to the question.

Testimonial.title: Testimonial
Testimonial.description: Represents a customer testimonial or review.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

authorName.description: The name of the person providing the testimonial.

authorRoleOrTitle.description: The role or title of the author (e.g., 'Cliente Satisfeita').

content.description: The text content of the testimonial.

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the testimonial was submitted.

definition.description: Stores individual blog posts. All documents in this collection are publicly readable. Includes flags like 'isHeroCarouselItem' and 'isFeaturedPost' for display purposes, allowing filtered queries. Admin users have write access.

params.name: blogPostId
params.description: Unique identifier for the blog post.

definition.description: Stores information about blog post authors. All documents are publicly readable. Admin users have write access.
Stores categories for blog posts and products. All documents are publicly readable. Includes 'isFeatured' flag for display. Admin users have write access.
Stores product items for the catalog showcase. All documents are publicly readable. Includes 'isCatalogItem' flag for display. Admin users have write access.
Stores static content pages like 'About Us' or 'Contact'. All documents are publicly readable. Queryable by 'slug'. Admin users have write access.

params.name: staticPageId
params.description: Unique identifier for the static page.

definition.description: Stores Frequently Asked Questions and their answers. All documents are publicly readable. Admin users have write access.
Stores customer testimonials. All documents are publicly readable. Admin users have write access.

A collection storing documents where the ID is an authenticated user's UID, indicating administrative privileges. Used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to grant write access to content collections. Only authenticated users with their UID present here can perform admin actions. Read access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == uid`).

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication User ID of an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be flat and public-facing for content entities, prioritizing simple and robust security rules. All core content (BlogPosts, Authors, Categories, Products, StaticPages, FAQs, Testimonials) resides in top-level collections, providing homogeneous security postures where all documents within a collection are publicly readable. Write access to these collections will be restricted to authenticated administrators, managed via a dedicated `/roles_admin` collection.

Authorization Independence is achieved by keeping a flat structure for publicly consumable content. Since there are no nested entities with complex, inherited authorization requirements (e.g., a subcollection whose access depends on its parent's `members` map), `get()` calls in security rules for authorization are entirely avoided. For instance, a `BlogPost`'s `authorId` is stored directly within the `BlogPost` document itself, eliminating the need to read the `Author` document to determine if the current user is the author (though for this public blog, read access is universal).

QAPs (Queries as Permissions) are supported by segregating data into logically distinct, top-level collections. For example, all `BlogPost` entities are in `/blogPosts`. The application can then query this collection using `where('isHeroCarouselItem', '==', true)` for the hero carousel or `where('isFeaturedPost', '==', true)` for featured posts. The security rule for `/blogPosts` simply grants public read access, ensuring that any authorized read operation (including `list`) returns only data permitted for the request. The filtering logic happens at the query level, not within the security rules, thus maintaining QAP integrity. Similarly, `Categories` and `Products` use `isFeatured` and `isCatalogItem` flags for client-side filtering after public read access is granted to their respective collections. Administrative roles are handled by checking `request.auth.uid` against the `/roles_admin` collection, a standard DBAC pattern.
